summary: The CI audit job failed because `pnpm audit` detected a known moderate-severity vulnerability in the `ip-address` package (XSS in Address6 HTML-emitting methods). The vulnerable version (<=10.1.0) is present in the dependency chain and a patched version (>=10.1.1) exists. `pnpm audit` exited with code 1, causing the job to fail.
root_cause: A known moderate vulnerability (GHSA-v2v4-37r5-5v8g) exists in `ip-address` <=10.1.0 within the dependency path `network__fetch>@pnpm/network.agent>@pnpm/network.proxy-agent>socks-proxy-agent>socks>ip-address`. The `pnpm audit` command exits non-zero when vulnerabilities are found, failing the CI job.

evidence.quote: ip-address has XSS in Address6 HTML-emitting methods
evidence.reason: This is the exact vulnerability description reported by pnpm audit, confirming the nature of the security issue.
Confirms the installed version of ip-address is vulnerable.
A fix is available, indicating the dependency needs to be updated to >=10.1.1.
Shows the exact transitive dependency path through which the vulnerable ip-address version is pulled in.
pnpm audit summary confirming a single moderate vulnerability was detected.
pnpm audit returned exit code 1 due to found vulnerabilities, which caused the CI job to fail.

suggested_fix: Update the `ip-address` dependency (directly or via its parent `socks`) to a version >=10.1.1 to resolve the XSS vulnerability (GHSA-v2v4-37r5-5v8g). Check if `socks` or `socks-proxy-agent` have released updates that bump `ip-address` to the patched version, then update the lockfile accordingly.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
